string.text: Side Saddle sized for shotgun shells. It makes reloading faster on weapons that dont rely on magazines.
Side Saddle sized for rifle rounds. It makes reloading faster on weapons that dont rely on magazines.
Standard .45 ACP Vector magazine with a capacity of 30 rounds.
Extneded Glock magazine for 9x19 Parabellum cartridges with a capacity of 33 rounds.
.45 ACP Vector drum magazine with a capacity of 45 rounds.
Glock drum magazine for 9x19 Parabellum cartridges with a capacity of 50 rounds.
Standard-issue Beretta 92F Magazine that holds 17 rounds.
Standard PX4 Storm Magazine. The magazine has a slightly different feeding lip design which makes it incompatible with the 92F.
Standard Remington 700 Magazine with a capacity of 5 rounds.
Standard STANAG Pattern Magazine for 7.62x39 ammo that holds 30 rounds.
Standard PPSh41 Drum that hold 71 rounds of 7.62x25 ammo.
Standard PPSh41 stick magazine that hold 35 rounds of 7.62x25 ammo. These are much easier to carry in your pockets than the drum magazine.
20-round PMAG style magazine for STANAG pattern rifles.
20-round PMAG style magazine for STANAG pattern rifles modified to feed 5.45x39 rounds. Why anyone would modify a rifle in this way is anyone's guess.

An explanation of how combat reloading (reloading with inventory closed) works:




With this option off, you must have a free slot available in your loadout of the appropriate magazine size for it to return to your loadout. If no appropriately sized slot is found, the magazine will be returned to your inventory.
 

With the option on, Magazines will cycle in and out of your inventory without the need for an appropriately sized slot to be available.
Place an ejected magazine back into your loadout even if you have no free slots.
